Prism Analytics Labs

Turning data chaos into actionable intelligence for businesses everywhere

MID DATA SCIENTIST

San Francisco, CA (hybrid) · Full-time · $115,000 - $145,000 per year

Prism Analytics Labs is seeking a Mid Data Scientist to join our growing Analytics Platform team. In this

role, you'll design and implement machine learning models, conduct rigorous A/B tests and

experimentation analyses, and collaborate with product and engineering teams to ship data-driven

features. You'll work on both experimental infrastructure and production models serving millions of

events daily. This is an ideal role for someone with 2-5 years of professional data science experience

who wants to deepen their expertise in experimentation, statistical modeling, and model deployment.

ABOUT US

Prism Analytics Labs is a fast-growing data intelligence platform founded in 2019. We partner with mid-

market and enterprise companies across retail, fintech, and healthcare to build scalable data pipelines,
advanced predictive models, and experimentation platforms. Our mission is to democratize data

science by making sophisticated analytics accessible to teams without deep technical expertise. We're
a team of 120+ data scientists, engineers, and product specialists, and we're expanding our analytics

division.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Design, implement, and deploy supervised and unsupervised machine learning models using
Python (scikit-learn, XGBoost) for classification, regression, and clustering tasks on datasets with

millions of rows

Own the end-to-end experimentation pipeline: design A/B tests and multi-armed bandit
experiments, calculate statistical power, analyze results using appropriate statistical methods, and
communicate findings to stakeholders

Write optimized SQL queries to extract, transform, and aggregate data from PostgreSQL and
Snowflake data warehouses for exploratory data analysis and model training

Perform exploratory data analysis using Python (Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, Seaborn) to identify
patterns, validate hypotheses, and generate insights that inform product decisions

Collaborate with product managers and backend engineers to translate business questions into
analytical experiments and ensure proper instrumentation and logging

Develop and maintain documentation for models, data pipelines, and experimentation

methodologies; present findings and recommendations to both technical and non-technical
audiences

 Participate in code reviews, contribute to best practices documentation, and mentor junior

analysts or data science interns on statistical concepts and modeling techniques

Monitor model performance in production, implement retraining pipelines, and investigate model

degradation issues

REQUIRED

2-5 years of professional experience in data science, analytics engineering, or quantitative roles

Strong proficiency in Python for data manipulation, statistical analysis, and machine learning

(Pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn)

Solid SQL skills; ability to write complex queries to extract and aggregate data efficiently from

relational databases

Hands-on experience designing and analyzing A/B tests, experiments, and statistical hypothesis

testing; understanding of concepts like power analysis, multiple testing corrections, and effect

sizes

Familiarity with machine learning fundamentals: supervised learning (linear/logistic regression,
tree-based models), model evaluation, cross-validation, and feature engineering

Comfortable with version control systems (Git) and collaborative development practices

NICE TO HAVE

Experience with experimentation platforms (e.g., Statsig, LaunchDarkly, or custom in-house
systems)

Familiarity with cloud data platforms (Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks) and ETL tools

Knowledge of causal inference techniques or quasi-experimental methods (propensity score
matching, difference-in-differences)

Experience deploying models with frameworks like FastAPI, Flask, or containerization (Docker)

TECH STACK

Python (Pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn)

  SQL (PostgreSQL, Snowflake)

  A/B Testing & Experimentation

Statistical Analysis & Hypothesis Testing   XGBoost / LightGBM   Matplotlib / Seaborn   Jupyter Notebooks   Git / GitHub

Apache Spark (PySpark)

  Statsmodels

BENEFITS

Competitive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with 90% premium coverage

401(k) matching up to 4% of salary

Unlimited PTO with a minimum of 15 days encouraged annually

Learning and development budget of $2,000 per year for courses, conferences, or certifications

Weekly catered lunches and fully stocked kitchen with snacks and beverages

Home office equipment stipend ($1,500 for hybrid workers) and standing desk options

 Parental leave (12 weeks paid for primary caregivers, 8 weeks for secondary)

Mental health support including free counseling sessions and meditation app subscriptions

HOW TO APPLY

Apply directly through our careers portal at prism-analytics-labs.com/careers or submit your resume and

a brief cover letter to careers@prism-analytics-labs.com. In your application, please include a link to

your GitHub profile or a portfolio of past data science projects. We review applications on a rolling basis

and aim to respond within 1-2 weeks.
